summary: I read both design guides (`guides/backend-guide.md`, `guides/ui-style-guide.md`) and checked the Dashboard UI under review — the `Dashboard::*` query objects and `Broadcast` service, `HomeController`/`PhasesController`, the `home/*` views and Stimulus controllers, the eight service broadcast call-sites, and the in-PR guide/artifact-schema additions — independently re-verifying each item against the current code rather than echoing the prior pass. The implementation is strongly aligned: business logic lives in POROs/query objects, controllers are thin and branch only on `result.success?`/format, broadcasts fire from services after commit targeting the smallest DOM unit via a per-user stream, progress ticks correctly opt out of the dashboard fan-out, the shared `status_badge` helper carries semantic tones with the word always shown, and the two genuinely new patterns (presentation-boundary rescue, cross-pipeline per-user stream) were added to both guides in the same PR as required — so no blockers or majors. I emitted a **needs_work** verdict with the same five minor, still-unaddressed deviations, all re-confirmed present in the current files: off-scale `red-500`/`green-500` fills vs the reserved `-600` scale (F1), a hand-rolled attention pill that bypasses the single-source `status_badge` helper (F2), run-selection + JSON-parsing domain logic sitting in a view helper and duplicating existing code (F3), three empty states missing the guide-mandated primary action (F4), and a poll-driven fleet panel that departs from the stream-first convention without a sanctioning guide note (F5) — each cited to its specific guide rule and written to `verdict.json`.
